questions.question: Kharg Island, that was recently seen in news, is a small coral island located in which country?
questions.explanation: Kharg Island is a small coral island in Iran in the northern Persian Gulf, recently discussed in United States strategic debates. It is one of the few Persian Gulf islands with natural freshwater, stored in porous limestone formations. The island has hot, humid summers and its highest point is Mount Didehban. In the early 1960s, discovery of offshore oil fields led to major petroleum and petrochemical infrastructure development. Its strategic importance comes from its location near the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global oil shipping route.

questions.question: The National Climate Stack Innovation Challenge has been launched by which organization?

options.label: B
options.text: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD)

answer.label: B
answer.text: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD)

questions.explanation: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD), with Gates Foundation and Dalberg Advisors, launched the National Climate Stack Innovation Challenge to strengthen India’s rural climate resilience. The initiative aims to build a National Climate Stack, integrating fragmented climate datasets into a unified digital platform. It will develop 10–15 year climate hazard forecasting frameworks for risks like heatwaves, floods, droughts, and cyclones affecting agriculture. The project will also create practical dashboards for agriculture, rural finance, and public planning decisions.

questions.question: India’s first fully operational ‘Ring Metro’ was inaugurated in which city?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated two new Delhi Metro corridors, including India’s first fully operational ‘Ring Metro’, improving public transport in the capital. The first corridor, Majlis Park–Maujpur Babarpur on the Pink Line, is 12.3 km long with eight elevated stations and forms part of the Majlis Park–Shiv Vihar Pink Line, extending it to 71.56 km. The second corridor, Deepali Chowk–Majlis Park on the Magenta Line, is 9.9 km long with seven elevated stations, raising the line length to 49 km; parts reach 28.36 metres height, among the highest in Delhi Metro.

questions.question: What is the name of campaign launched by the Ministry of Panchayati Raj to combat proxy leadership in Gram Panchayats?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Panchayati Raj (MoPR), India launched the ‘Say No To Proxy Sarpanch’ social media campaign to address the practice of proxy leadership in Gram Panchayats. The campaign, coinciding with International Women’s Day, runs until 18 March 2026 and encourages citizens to celebrate real women Sarpanches and speak against the “Sarpanch Pati Culture.” It targets situations where elected women leaders are sidelined, and male relatives like husbands (“Sarpanch Patis”) exercise authority in their place. The initiative seeks to raise nationwide awareness, amplify village voices, and promote authentic grassroots leadership.

questions.question: Sinhagad Fort, that was recently seen in news, is located in which state?
questions.explanation: Recently, about 25 visitors were injured after a swarm of bees attacked tourists at Sinhagad Fort, bringing the historic site into news. Sinhagad, earlier known as Kondhana, is an ancient hill fortress located near Pune, Maharashtra, in the Sahyadri range and is believed to be around 2,000 years old. The fort was captured in 1340 CE by Muhammad bin Tughlaq and later controlled by the Nizam Shahi dynasty. Shivaji captured the fort in 1647, but it was handed to the Mughals under the Treaty of Purandar. It was recaptured in 1670 by Tanaji Malusare during the Battle of Sinhagad, where he sacrificed his life. After this victory, the fort was renamed Sinhagad (Lion’s Fort).

questions.question: Shailesh Kumar won which medal in men’s high jump T63 at the World Para Athletics Grand Prix 2026?
questions.explanation: Shailesh Kumar won gold medal in men’s high jump T63 at the World Para Athletics Grand Prix, New Delhi. He achieved a best jump of 1.84 m, below his personal best of 1.91 m, but was satisfied as it was the season opener. Shailesh hails from Bihar and targets the Asian Para Games, focusing his training program on that event. At the 2025 World Championships, Shailesh attempted 1.94 m thrice, exceeding the championship record but missing the world record.

questions.question: Which international organization has declared 2026 as the International Year of the Woman Farmer (IYWF)?
questions.explanation: The United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) has declared 2026 as the International Year of the Woman Farmer (IYWF 2026). A workforce shift is occurring as rural men move to non-farm jobs, leading women to increasingly take on agricultural roles. Women’s participation in agriculture has surged by 135% in a decade, now constituting 42% of the agricultural workforce. Two in three working women are engaged in agriculture, though this remains lower than global averages of 57%-63%. In 2023-24, there were 117.6 million women in agriculture: 21.7 million hired, 95.1 million self-employed, and 0.8 million regular workers.

questions.question: Operation Sagar Bandhu was launched by India to provide assistance to which country?
questions.explanation: The Indian Army has launched a major bridge construction project in Sri Lanka under Operation Sagar Bandhu. The bridge will connect the capital city Colombo with the economic centre Puttalam, along an important coastal corridor passing through Chilaw District. Operation Sagar Bandhu was launched by India to provide urgent Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief (HADR) to Sri Lanka in late November 2025. The operation was initiated following the devastation caused by Cyclone Ditwah.

questions.question: Lower Kopili Hydroelectric Project is located in which state?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister inaugurated the Lower Kopili Hydroelectric Project, a 120 megawatt (MW) run-of-river hydropower project. It is located in Assam in the West Karbi Anglong and Dima Hasao Autonomous District Council areas. The project is developed and owned by Assam Power Generation Corporation Limited. It is funded by the Asian Development Bank under the Assam Power Sector Investment Program. The project is built on the Kopili River basin. Kopili River is an interstate river flowing through Meghalaya and Assam.

questions.question: What kind of disease is “Hutchinson-Gilford Progeria Syndrome (HGPS)” that was recently seen in news?
questions.explanation: United States-based Sentynl Therapeutics, part of Zydus Lifesciences, has partnered with South Korea’s PRG S&T to develop Progerinin, a drug for Hutchinson-Gilford Progeria Syndrome (HGPS). Hutchinson-Gilford Progeria Syndrome (HGPS) is an extremely rare genetic disorder that causes rapid aging in children. The disease is caused by a mutation in the LMNA (Lamin A) gene, which normally produces lamin A protein for maintaining cell structure. This mutation leads to the formation of an abnormal protein called progerin, which damages the cell nucleus. The damaged cells die early, resulting in premature aging of the body.
